body:
🔴 **`getClientHealthCategory` can never return "Yeni Kazanılan" despite declaring it in the return type** The `getClientHealthCategory` function declares a return type of `"VIP" | "Riskli" | "Yeni Kazanılan" | "Normal"` but its implementation can never return `"Yeni Kazanılan"`. Any score ≥ 85 returns "VIP", any score ≤ 45 returns "Riskli", and everything else returns "Normal".
Root Cause The function at `lib/calculations/client-health.ts:65-68` has no branch that produces the `"Yeni Kazanılan"` value: ```typescript export function getClientHealthCategory(score: number): "VIP" | "Riskli" | "Yeni Kazanılan" | "Normal" { if (score >= 85) return "VIP"; if (score <= 45) return "Riskli"; return "Normal"; } ``` The `ClientHealthRecord` type at `components/admin/analytics/client-health-table.tsx:14` includes `"Yeni Kazanılan"` as a valid segment, and mock data at `app/(dashboard)/admin/analytics/page.tsx:36` uses this segment. When this function is used to derive a client's segment from a computed health score (which is its stated purpose), new clients will never be categorized as "Yeni Kazanılan" and will instead be misclassified as "Normal" or another category. **Impact:** Any consumer relying on this function to segment clients will never see the "Yeni Kazanılan" category, breaking the intended 4-tier segmentation model.
Prompt for agents ``` In lib/calculations/client-health.ts, the getClientHealthCategory function (lines 65-68) needs an additional condition to return "Yeni Kazanılan". The function currently only handles VIP (score >= 85), Riskli (score <= 45), and defaults to Normal. A branch for "Yeni Kazanılan" needs to be added. The exact score threshold depends on business requirements - one approach would be to add a second parameter (e.g., serviceDurationMonths) and return "Yeni Kazanılan" for clients with short service duration, or to use a specific score range. For example, the function signature could be updated to accept additional context, or a simple score-based threshold could be added between Riskli and Normal. ```
--- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🚩 **Health score calculation uses filtered operational scores (zeros excluded)** At `components/admin/analytics/client-health-table.tsx:120`, operational scores with value 0 are filtered out before computing the health score: ``` const opScoresNum = record.operationalScores.filter(s => s.score > 0).map(s => s.score); ``` For the mock client "Moda Mania" with scores `[{SEO: 0}, {SEM: 76}]`, only `[76]` is passed to `calculateClientHealthScore`. This means the average operational score is 76 (just SEM), not 38 (average of 0 and 76). This appears intentional — a score of 0 likely means "service not yet started" rather than "service performing at 0%" (the table also shows "--" for zero scores at line 106). However, this creates a subtle semantic difference: a client with one non-started service looks operationally healthier than a client with two mediocre services. Worth confirming this is the desired behavior. --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*
